title: parameter-definitions
description: Central registry of all UI/template parameter definitions. Templates reference parameters by id only; the canonical definition lives here. The id is the object key under `parameters` — uniqueness is intrinsic.

parameters.description: Map of parameter id → definition. Keys must match ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_.-]*$ and are intrinsically unique.

name.description: Human-readable label shown in the UI.

description.description: Help text shown as tooltip or expandable help panel. Optional in JSON; if missing, the loader falls back to the matching `## <id>` section in parameter-definitions.md (parallel file).

internal.description: Never shown in the UI. Resolved by backend, by application properties, or by addons.

project.description: Marks parameters that are typically set once globally per project (DNS, mirrors, registry credentials, …) rather than per application. Pure documentation hint — no runtime effect, used by the project-settings doc generator.

advanced.description: Hidden behind the Advanced toggle in the UI.

tasks.description: If set, the parameter only appears in the UI form for these tasks. Default (unset): visible for every task. Use 'installation' to mark fields that are configured once at install and inherited unchanged on reconfigure/upgrade.

secure.description: Marks sensitive values (PATs, passwords, secrets) — UI masks input and avoids logging.

certtype.description: Certificate type. Enables auto-generation when upload has no value.

enumValuesTemplate.description: Template name that produces the enum values at runtime (only valid for type:enum).

enumValues.description: Static enum values (only valid for type:enum).

if.description: Parameter id or special condition (e.g. env_file_has_markers). When falsy, the parameter is hidden in the UI.

group.description: Logical UI section. Required for non-internal parameters.

order.description: Sort order within the group. Lower = higher up. Default 100.

allOf.$comment: internal and advanced are mutually exclusive
type:enum requires either enumValues or enumValuesTemplate
